note: Submodule of varnish_purger (project varnish_purge). Purges image-style derivatives from Varnish when a focal_point crop entity updates, via URIBAN requests. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing documented facts; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: When does the Varnish Focal Point Purger submodule purge images, and what does it purge?
evals.expected_output: When a focal_point crop entity is updated, it bans every image-style derivative of the cropped file in Varnish via URIBAN requests.

evals.prompt: How is Varnish Focal Point Purger configured?
evals.expected_output: It has no configuration or admin page — just enable it (drush en varnish_focal_point_purge). It reuses an existing varnish purger's hostname/port.

evals.prompt: Which HTTP request method must Varnish handle for Varnish Focal Point Purger?
evals.expected_output: URIBAN — the VCL must handle the URIBAN method (ban on host + url, return synthetic 200).

evals.prompt: What is the difference between varnish_focal_point_purge and varnish_image_purge?
evals.expected_output: varnish_focal_point_purge reacts only to focal_point crop-entity updates, while varnish_image_purge reacts to updates of general content entities and purges their image-field derivatives.
